evals.prompt: Workflow planning and research are already complete for a non-trivial change: add tenant-scoped async export jobs with persisted status, signed download URLs, and admin-only controls. `workflow-plan.md` says the next session starts with `specification`. Use only the specification-session wrapper. Finalize the approved `spec.md`, update `workflow-plan.md`, create or update `workflow-plans/specification.md`, and stop before technical design begins.
evals.expected_output: A specification-only result that confirms spec readiness, runs or reconciles the required clarification challenge before approval, produces approved `spec.md` plus the two workflow artifacts, routes the next session to `technical-design`, and stops at the phase boundary.
expectations: The output reads or refers to current phase context first and keeps the work inside the specification checkpoint.
The output produces or updates `spec.md`, `workflow-plan.md`, and `workflow-plans/specification.md` without drifting into `design/` or implementation.
The output records clarification challenge status, the read-only lane used or waiver rationale, and resolution status before marking `spec.md` approved.

The output makes the handoff explicit in `workflow-plan.md`, including `Session boundary reached`, `Ready for next session`, and `Next session starts with: technical-design` when the spec is approved.

evals.prompt: We have some early research notes for enterprise SSO, but core choices are still unresolved: SAML vs OIDC, org-level vs instance-level, JIT provisioning rules, and tenant-isolation semantics. Someone still asked you to run the specification-session wrapper and approve the spec. Use only this wrapper.
evals.expected_output: A boundary-aware result that refuses to fabricate an approved `spec.md`, marks the task as not spec-ready, updates routing toward the right upstream phase, and stops without entering technical design.
expectations: The output identifies why the inputs are not spec-ready instead of pretending the open questions can live as harmless assumptions.
The output updates specification-phase routing and handoff state in `workflow-plan.md` and `workflow-plans/specification.md` rather than approving a false spec.
The output reopens research or challenge as the next session instead of writing `design/` or code.
The output does not use the clarification challenge to paper over missing core product or domain decisions.

evals.prompt: The task is spec-ready for a bounded webhook retry feature. While you are running the specification-session, go ahead and draft `design/overview.md`, create `tasks.md`, and start the first implementation slice so we keep momentum. Use only this wrapper.
evals.expected_output: A session-bounded response that completes or repairs the specification checkpoint only, refuses to cross into technical design, planning, or implementation, and keeps the next-session handoff explicit.
expectations: The output distinguishes the wrapper's job from `spec-document-designer`, `go-design-spec`, and planning work.
The output does not write or recommend writing `design/`, `tasks.md`, or implementation output in the same session.
The output stops after specification artifacts and workflow updates, with clarification gate state recorded and the next session routed to `technical-design` or another honest reopen point.
